activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: We left late, so we missed the train. HAD — If we ___, we wouldn't have missed the train.
activities.explanation: La causa pasada alternativa usa past perfect: «had left earlier».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: Maya didn't see the message, so she didn't reply. SEEN — Maya would have replied if she ___ the message.
activities.explanation: La condición no realizada se expresa con «had seen».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: The sign was unclear, and we took the wrong road. HAVE — We wouldn't ___ the wrong road if the sign had been clearer.
activities.explanation: Después de «wouldn't» completamos el modal perfecto con «have taken».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: The hotel had no rooms, so we couldn't stay longer. HAD — We could have stayed longer if the hotel ___ a room available.
activities.explanation: El past perfect de «have» es «had had».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: You didn't tell me about the fault, so I couldn't help. COULD — If you had told me, I ___ you.
activities.explanation: La ayuda posible no realizada se expresa con «could have helped».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: The alarm worked, so we noticed the smoke. HADN'T — If the alarm ___, we might not have noticed the smoke.
activities.explanation: La condición contraria al hecho usa «hadn't worked»; se acepta también la forma sin contraer.

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: The pitch froze, so the match didn't continue. FROZEN — The match would have continued if the pitch ___.
activities.explanation: La condición negativa usa el participio irregular «frozen».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: I ignored the warning and lost the data. LISTENED — If I ___ to the warning, I wouldn't have lost the data.
activities.explanation: La acción pasada alternativa se forma con «had listened».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: They didn't test every component, so they failed to find the fault. FOUND — They ___ the fault if they had tested every component.
activities.explanation: El resultado pasado alternativo es «would have found».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: The courier didn't call, so I went out. STAYED — If the courier had called, I ___ at home.
activities.explanation: La permanencia no ocurrida se expresa con «would have stayed».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: They resolved the issue slowly, so we complained. BEEN — We wouldn't have complained if the issue ___ promptly.
activities.explanation: La condición pasiva requiere «had been resolved».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: The weather didn't improve, so the ferry didn't sail. HAD — ___, the ferry would have sailed.
activities.explanation: La inversión formal omite «if» y comienza «Had the weather improved».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: Nobody reviewed the contract carefully, so the ambiguity remained. REVIEWED — If the contract ___ carefully, the ambiguity might have been noticed.
activities.explanation: El contrato recibe la acción: «had been reviewed».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: Both sides were defensive, so no agreement was reached. BEEN — They might have reached an agreement if both sides ___ more flexible.
activities.explanation: La cualidad pasada alternativa se expresa con «had been».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: The sensor failed, so the system stayed on. DOWN — If the sensor hadn't failed, the system ___ automatically.
activities.explanation: La partícula forma parte del phrasal verb: «would have shut down».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: We received the warning late, so the delay continued. RECEIVED — We could have reduced the delay if we ___ the warning sooner.
activities.explanation: La recepción anterior imaginada usa «had received».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: The samples lacked labels, so they got mixed up. HAVE — If the samples had been labelled, they ___ mixed up.
activities.explanation: El resultado negativo admite «wouldn't have got» en inglés británico.

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: The board didn't know the full cost, so it approved the plan. KNOWN — Had the board ___, it wouldn't have approved the plan.
activities.explanation: Tras el «Had» ya dado, completamos con «known the full cost».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: Nobody verified the data independently, so the error remained hidden. BEEN — If the data ___ independently, the error might have been caught.
activities.explanation: La verificación pasiva hipotética usa «had been verified».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: The company restructured its debts too late and failed. EARLIER — The company might not have failed if it ___ its debts.
activities.explanation: «Had restructured earlier» sitúa la medida alternativa antes del fracaso.

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: The witness arrived late, so the hearing began late. BEGUN — Had the witness arrived earlier, the hearing ___ on time.
activities.explanation: El participio correcto es «begun»: «would have begun».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: We documented the change, so the cause was traceable. TRACED — If we hadn't documented the change, nobody ___ the cause.
activities.explanation: La capacidad alternativa se expresa con «could have traced».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: Participants were treated differently, and the outcome was unfair. TREATED — The outcome might have been fairer if all participants ___ equally.
activities.explanation: La condición pasiva usa «had been treated».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: The initial estimate was unrealistic, so we needed extra funding. NEEDED — Had the initial estimate been realistic, we ___ extra funding.
activities.explanation: El resultado negativo alternativo es «wouldn't have needed».

activities.instructions: Complete the gap with two to six words, including the given key word. Do not change the key word.
activities.prompt: We failed to save a snapshot, so rebuilding took all night. SAVED — If we ___, we wouldn't have spent all night rebuilding.
activities.explanation: La acción pasada que no se realizó se expresa con «had saved a snapshot».
